channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Zuby Ejiofor much more than just most improved in record-setting performance for No. 6 St. John's
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Maybe most improved didn't really do Zuby Ejiofor justice. The junior forward delivered an overpowering performance Friday night for No. 6 St.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Delta Girls Basketball punches ticket to 4A State Championship game
item.description: Saturday at the Denver Coliseum, the Delta Panthers girls basketball team will compete for their first-ever State Championship.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Ejiofor powers No. 6 St. John's past No. 25 Marquette 79-63 into 1st Big East final since 2000
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zuby Ejiofor scored a career-high 33 points, a St. John's record in the Big East Tournament, and the sixth-ranked Red Storm pulled away from No. 25 Marquette 79-63 on Friday night to reach the conference championship game for the first time in 25 years.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan basketball snaps 3-game skid, dominates Purdue to secure Big Ten Tournament semifinal spot
item.description: The No. 3 seeded Michigan Wolverines ended their three-game losing streak by taking down the Purdue Boilermakers 86-68 to advance to the Big Ten Tournament semi-finals.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: F1 hometown driver Piastri fastest in final practice ahead of Australian GP qualifying
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — McLaren’s hometown hero Oscar Piastri led a super-competitive final practice session Saturday for Sunday's Formula 1 Australian Grand Prix with the Australian just 39 one-thousandths of a second ahead of Mercedes’ George Russell.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: No. 96 fits Hurricanes' Roslovic just right as he scores goal 2nd straight game
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Jack Roslovic got his No. 96 jersey back, and he's wearing it well. The Carolina Hurricanes center scored a goal in a second straight game and he has four points over three games since reclaiming the number.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Withers' lane violation adds improbable twist to UNC's failed comeback against No. 1 Duke in ACCs
item.description: North Carolina graduate forward Jae'Lyn Withers committed a lane violation with 4.1 seconds left that negated a tying free throw in the Tar Heels' loss to No. 1 Duke in the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No. 1 Duke holds off furious North Carolina rally to win 74-71, reach ACC title game without Flagg
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Kon Knueppel scored 17 points and No. 1 Duke held off a furious second half rally by North Carolina to beat its rival for the third time this season, 74-71 on Friday night to reach the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament championship game.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jourdan Lewis becomes latest former Cowboys player to voice frustration with team
item.description: Jacksonville Jaguars defensive back Jourdan Lewis said he experienced moments of frustration during his eight-year stint with the Dallas Cowboys.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Maliq Brown miss No. 1 Duke's win against rival UNC in ACC semifinals
item.description: Top-ranked Duke played without Cooper Flagg and Maliq Brown in its win against rival North Carolina in the Atlantic Conference Tournament because of injuries.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lakers' James, Hachimura, Hayes fly back to California for treatment and will miss rest of road trip
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — LeBron James flew back to California for treatment on his left groin strain and will miss the rest of the Los Angeles Lakers’ four-game trip, which concludes Friday at Denver.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rory McIlroy mum on video of him taking phone from University of Texas player heckling him
item.description: PONTE VEDRA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Rory McIlroy was interested only in his good start to The Players Championship on Thursday than discussing a heckling incident in which he took the phone of a University of Texas player who had heckled him during a practice round.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Grand Valley's Muay Thai team showed they are not to be messed with
item.description: Out of 36 competing teams from all across the Mountain West, the boys from the Valley finished third.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Warriors' Steph Curry becomes investor in Unrivaled Basketball
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Stephen Curry has joined Unrivaled basketball as an investor. The Golden State Warriors guard is the latest sports star to join the 3v3 basketball league as an investor, joining the likes of the NBA's Giannis Antetokounmpo and Carmelo Anthony, and tennis star Coco Gauff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: DK Metcalf is leaving Seattle for Pittsburgh with a new deal in tow, AP sources say
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks are sending two-time Pro Bowler D.K. Metcalf to Pittsburgh for at least a second-round pick, sources with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL gives up two stoppage time goals in 3-1 loss
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After jumping out to an early lead, Real Salt Lake allowed three straight goals, two in stoppage time, and loses in its first meeting with San Diego FC Saturday night at America First Field, 3-1.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Giddey scores 26 to lead Bulls' comeback from 17 down to beat the Heat 114-109
item.description: Josh Giddey scored 26 points and the Chicago Bulls rallied from a 17-point, first-half deficit to beat the Miami Heat 114-109 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tatum scores 40, Celtics beat Lakers 111-101 after James leaves late with groin injury
item.description: Jayson Tatum had 40 points, 12 rebounds, and eight assists, and the Boston Celtics held off a late rally to beat the Los Angeles Lakers 111-101 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Daytona 500 winner William Byron claims NASCAR Cup pole
item.description: On Saturday, Daytona 500 winner William Byron claimed the Busch Light pole during the 312-mile race at Phoenix Raceway. According to NASCAR, it's the 14th pole of the 27 year-old's career.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Aggies dominate Air Force in regular season finale
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Mason Falslev scored 18 points on 8-of-10 shooting to lead the Utah State basketball team to a dominating 87-47 win over Air Force. The Aggies had lost two in a row for the first time all year, but gave their fans plenty to cheer about in their home finale.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Amber Whiting resigns as BYU women's basketball coach
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU women's basketball coach Amber Whiting announced she is resigning after three seasons in Provo. The decision to part ways was mutual between Whiting and the school. Assistant coach Lee Cummard will serve as the interim head coach for now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
